articles.title: Argentina blocks access to Polymarket after early bets on February inflation
articles.summary: Argentina's government blocked access to Polymarket after users began placing early bets on February inflation data. The move signals that countries will crack down on prediction markets when they threaten to expose uncomfortable economic realities before official announcements.

articles.title: Capitol News Illinois | Illinois regulators say prediction markets are illegal gambling, but bettors — and the Trump family — love them
articles.summary: Illinois regulators just declared prediction markets illegal gambling, putting the state at odds with the growing betting trend that's attracted everyone from retail traders to the Trump family. This sets up a potential clash between state gambling laws and the federal approval prediction markets received earlier this year, which could create a patchwork of legal uncertainty across different states.

articles.title: Prediction markets and investable controversy: Opportunity or overreach?
articles.summary: Prediction markets are expanding beyond politics and sports into controversial topics that investors can actually trade, raising questions about whether we're crossing ethical lines. Smart money sees untapped alpha in society's messiest debates, but regulators and platforms are scrambling to figure out where speculation ends and exploitation begins.

articles.title: March Madness Outlook on Kalshi: Teams With the Highest Probability to Win the Title
articles.summary: Kalshi's March Madness markets are pricing in clear favorites for the NCAA tournament championship, with certain teams commanding significantly higher win probabilities than traditional sportsbooks might suggest. These odds reveal where smart money is flowing and could expose value plays for bettors willing to fade the crowd or identify teams the prediction market is undervaluing.

articles.title: Prediction market Kalshi takes legal stand against Arizona’s gambling laws
articles.summary: Kalshi is challenging Arizona's gambling laws in court, presumably to defend its prediction market operations in the state. If successful, the case could set important legal precedent for how prediction markets are regulated nationwide, potentially opening doors in other restrictive jurisdictions.

articles.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Got the Oscars About 80 Percent Right
articles.summary: Prediction markets Polymarket and Kalshi correctly predicted roughly 80% of Oscar winners this year, showing their growing accuracy in entertainment forecasting. The strong performance validates that betting markets can cut through Hollywood hype and insider politics to identify actual frontrunners, making them valuable tools for future awards season gambling.

articles.title: Make the Predictions Come True
articles.summary: Some prediction markets are shifting from pure forecasting to actively trying to influence the outcomes they're betting on. This blurs the line between predicting and manipulating events, which could either supercharge market accuracy or completely undermine their credibility as information sources.

articles.title: Perfection is nearly impossible: Kalshi is giving away $1 billion for a perfect bracket
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering $1 billion to anyone who can correctly predict every game in March Madness, joining the long tradition of impossible bracket challenges that generate massive publicity while costing almost nothing. The stunt shows how prediction markets are using lottery-style promotions to attract mainstream attention, even though the mathematical odds make Warren Buffett's old $1 billion bracket bet look generous.

articles.title: Polymarket bettors send death threats to reporter over missile report tied to $14 million prediction market pool
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are threatening to kill a journalist who reported on missile strikes that apparently moved a $14 million prediction market. The incident shows how high-stakes betting can turn normally rational market participants into unhinged lunatics when their positions get torched by breaking news.

articles.title: Kalshi and Polymarket correctly predicted 19 out of 24 Oscar winners. Here are the ones they missed.
articles.summary: Prediction markets Kalshi and Polymarket nailed 79% of Oscar winners this year, missing only 5 categories out of 24. The strong performance adds more credibility to betting markets as entertainment forecasting tools, especially as Hollywood awards become increasingly predictable through industry sentiment and campaign spending.

articles.title: JPMorgan Is Considering New Prediction Market Guidance for Employees
articles.summary: JPMorgan is developing internal policies around employee participation in prediction markets, likely triggered by the recent surge in political betting and regulatory uncertainty. This signals that major financial institutions are taking prediction markets seriously enough to create formal guidance, which could legitimize the space and potentially restrict how Wall Street professionals engage with these platforms.

articles.title: Gamblers trying to win a bet on Polymarket are vowing to kill me if I don’t rewrite an Iran missile story
articles.summary: Someone placed bets on Polymarket tied to specific wording in an Iran missile attack story, and now they're sending death threats to pressure the reporter into changing the article to make their bet win. This shows how prediction markets can create perverse incentives where bettors try to manipulate the underlying events or reporting rather than just predicting outcomes.
